name: error-debugging-error-trace
description: You are an error tracking and observability expert specializing in implementing comprehensive error monitoring solutions. Set up error tracking systems, configure alerts, implement structured logging, and ensure teams can quickly identify and resolve production issues.
capabilities: Set up Sentry SDK for Node.js, Python, and web applications
Configure error grouping, severity levels, and triage workflows
Implement structured logging with appropriate context
Create alert rules and notification routing for different error types
Add custom error fingerprinting for better deduplication
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 35 static findings are false positives. The backtick characters flagged are JavaScript template literals, not shell execution. Network calls are legitimate error tracking API communications. Environment access patterns are configuration objects, not secret exfiltration. This is a legitimate error monitoring implementation guide.
valueStatement: Production errors go unnoticed without proper monitoring. This skill helps you set up Sentry and other error tracking systems to detect, triage, and resolve issues before users complain.
limitations: Requires application code access for SDK integration
Cannot retroactively capture errors before SDK installation
May require paid plans for advanced features on hosted services
Does not replace manual code debugging for complex issues

useCases.title: Add error tracking to a new Node.js API
useCases.description: Set up Sentry from scratch for a REST API, configure source maps, and create alerts for 5xx errors

useCases.title: Improve existing error handling
useCases.description: Analyze current error patterns, implement structured logging, and reduce noise from unactionable errors

useCases.title: Create observability dashboard
useCases.description: Build a real-time error monitoring dashboard with trend analysis and team alerting workflows

promptTemplates.title: Basic Sentry Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up Sentry error tracking for my Node.js application. The project is in /app and uses Express. Configure it to capture unhandled rejections and exceptions with environment 'production'.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick setup for a new project

promptTemplates.title: Configure Error Grouping
promptTemplates.prompt: Our Sentry dashboard has too many duplicate errors. Set up custom fingerprinting to group similar errors together based on the error message and stack trace pattern. Show me how to configure this in the SDK.

promptTemplates.title: Alert Routing Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure Sentry alerts for our team. Route critical errors (5xx responses) to Slack #incidents, warnings to #engineering, and rate limits to #alerts-oncall. Use environment-based rules.

promptTemplates.title: Structured Logging Migration
promptTemplates.prompt: Migrate our console.log statements to structured logging compatible with Sentry. Include request ID, user ID, and operation name in the context for every log statement.

bestPractices: Always include user context (ID, email) when available to help debug user-specific issues
Use environment-based DSN configuration to separate production from development errors
Implement release tracking to correlate errors with specific deployments
antiPatterns: Capturing full request bodies without sanitizing sensitive fields like passwords
Setting sample rates too high during high-traffic periods causing bill spikes
Ignoring duplicate errors without proper fingerprinting configuration

faq.question: What languages and frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill covers Node.js, Python, and web JavaScript. Examples use Sentry but concepts apply to other services like Rollbar and Bugsnag.

faq.question: Do I need a Sentry account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a Sentry account and project DSN. The free tier covers 5k errors per month which is sufficient for small projects.

faq.question: Can this skill help with performance monitoring?
faq.answer: Yes, the implementation playbook includes performance monitoring setup with tracing and profiling configurations.

faq.question: How do I handle sensitive data in errors?
faq.answer: Use the beforeSend hook to sanitize events. The skill includes examples that filter password, token, and secret fields.

faq.question: Does this work with self-hosted error tracking?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use self-hosted Sentry or any service compatible with the Sentry SDK protocol by pointing to your own DSN.

faq.question: How do I debug errors that Sentry is not capturing?
faq.answer: Check that the SDK is initialized before other middleware, verify the DSN is correct, and ensure sample rates are not set to 0.
